events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario. Heavy industrial, motor circuits & disconnects, observed field condition: undersized motor overloads. Work staged. Need select correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice is wrong. Need decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? Undersized overloads would trip prematurely? Actually overloads undersized means heater elements or settings below motor FLA, causing nuisance tripping and potential inability to start motor. But could be safety? NEC Art 430 Part III requires overload protection sized per motor full-load current, service factor, temperature rise, etc. Undersized overloads are non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk maybe medium? Could cause production loss, motor starting issues, unnecessary downtime; not immediate hazard but non-compliant. However in industrial context, undersized overloads could lead to repeated starts/stops and equipment damage. Risk medium. Work_stage staged. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete 0. value 0. sound_source empty? Not audio. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings: overload heaters/settings below motor FLA/nameplate requirements; will cause nuisance tripping and violate NEC Art 430. actions: replace with correct overload heaters/coils/settings per motor nameplate FLA and NEC 430; verify starter heater selection chart; coordinate with engineering. rationale: overload protection must be sized to motor full-load current per NEC Art 430; undersized overloads trip below rated load, preventing motor from running and are non-compliant. references: NEC Art. 430 Part III (Overload Protection), specifically 430.31-430.44. Need valid JSON only. Ensure no prose outside JSON.
